title: MiFID II Best Execution — Venue Quality of Execution Report Ingestion Completeness

compliance.assurance_rationale: MiFID II Art.27 — UC provides continuous Splunk telemetry evidence for this regulatory requirement, uplifted from contributing to partial after review confirmed the SPL captures relevant monitoring signals.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that MiFID II Art.27 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.5.21: MiFID II Best Execution — Venue Quality of Execution Report Ingestion Completeness.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.5.21' running on index=compliance sourcetype="qoe:file_manifest" (venue, report_month, file_name, received_ts, expected_flag), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Universal Forwarder on SFTP landing zone, or S3 + SQS
spl:
index=compliance sourcetype="qoe:file_manifest" earliest=-400d
| stats latest(received_ts) as last_recv by venue, report_month
| eval expected=strptime(report_month,"%Y-%m")
| where isnull(last_recv) OR last_recv > relative_time(expected,"+35d@d")
| table venue, report_month, last_recv
description: RTS 27/28 obligations produce periodic reports. Detecting missing files per venue or late uploads demonstrates control over the reporting supply chain used to evidence best execution monitoring.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for MiFID II compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes compliance and sourcetypes qoe:file_manifest. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Adjust `+35d` to regulatory calendar; (2) alert compliance operations; (3) vendor SLA tracking; (4) store checksums; (5) map to annual best ex board review. Indexes required: `index=compliance`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=qoe:file_manifest`.
visualization: Table (missing/late), Heatmap venue x month, Single value (gaps).
knownFalsePositives: Reporting gaps during venue maintenance, scheduled feed restatements, or vendor data corrections.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Universal Forwarder on SFTP landing zone, or S3 + SQS.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=compliance` `sourcetype="qoe:file_manifest"` (venue, report_month, file_name, received_ts, expected_flag).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Adjust `+35d` to regulatory calendar; (2) alert compliance operations; (3) vendor SLA tracking; (4) store checksums; (5) map to annual best ex board review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=compliance sourcetype="qoe:file_manifest" earliest=-400d
| stats latest(received_ts) as last_recv by venue, report_month
| eval expected=strptime(report_month,"%Y-%m")
| where isnull(last_recv) OR last_recv > relative_time(expected,"+35d@d")
| table venue, report_month, last_recv
```

Understanding this SPL

**MiFID II Best Execution — Venue Quality of Execution Report Ingestion Completeness** — RTS 27/28 obligations produce periodic reports. Detecting missing files per venue or late uploads demonstrates control over the reporting supply chain used to evidence best execution monitoring.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=compliance` `sourcetype="qoe:file_manifest"` (venue, report_month, file_name, received_ts, expected_flag). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Universal Forwarder on SFTP landing zone, or S3 + SQS. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: compliance; **sourcetype**: qoe:file_manifest. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=compliance, sourcetype="qoe:file_manifest", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **venue, report_month**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **expected** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(last_recv) OR last_recv > relative_time(expected,"+35d@d")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: expected, last_recv, report_month, venue.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (missing/late), Heatmap venue x month, Single value (gaps).
